name: Curried Ham and Turkey (Usda)
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup butter, margarine, or turkey fat
recipeInstructions:
1. Line four 8x8x2-inch baking pans with heat-resistant freezer wrap, leaving enough extra to fold over the top. Use one pan for each six servings or one-fourth of the recipe. Do not line pans if food will be served without freezing.
2. Melt fat and stir in flour, salt, pepper, and curry powder. Add sweet red pepper flakes and onions.
3. Slowly stir in milk until thickened, stirring constantly. Stir in turkey and ham.
4. To serve without freezing: Heat to serving temperature after adding turkey and ham.
5. To freeze: Pour each fourth of the mixture into a baking pan. Pack food tightly to avoid air pockets. Cool for 30 minutes at room temperature. Wrap completely by pulling paper over the top, folding edges together several times, and sealing with freezer tape. Label with the food name, date frozen, and last usable date for best quality (about 6 months). Freeze for 10-12 hours before removing packages from pans.
6. To heat frozen curry: Preheat oven to 350°F (moderate). Remove freezer wrap and place food in a baking pan. Bake for 1 hour or until bubbly at the edges, stirring occasionally. Stir gently until sauce is smooth. Serve over hot toast or biscuits.
